title: plantago subulata subsp. capitellata
intro: Plantago subulata subsp. capitellata, also known as common plantain, is a perennial herb belonging to the plantain family and is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of plantain include reducing inflammation and swelling, treating wounds and skin irritations, and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Plantain can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
